name: opentrons-integration
tagline: Reference guide for writing Opentrons Protocol API v2 Python scripts to run OT-2/Flex lab robots (pipetting, modules, PCR).
verdict: Clean, purely educational reference skill for the official Opentrons robotics API. No credential access, network calls, shell execution, or hidden instructions found. Only minor polish gaps: file runs slightly over the ~500-line budget and doesn't call out that physical protocols (liquid handling, PCR) aren't safely re-runnable if a script is executed twice.

highlights.summary: At 570 lines with many near-duplicate code blocks per hardware module, the file runs long relative to the ~500-line guidance, costing extra context on every load.
No guidance on re-run safety for physical protocols — re-executing a generated script would re-dispense liquids/re-run PCR cycles on real hardware, which the skill doesn't flag as a risk to the user.
File is 570 lines, moderately over the ~500-line budget guidance.
